LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE UNDER C.C.P 436 IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF RELIES ON AN INAPPOSITE APPELLATE DOCTRINE. "AN APPELLATE COURT HAS THE INHERENT POWER, UNDER THE 'DISENTITLEMENT DOCTRINE,' TO DISMISS AN APPEAL BY A PARTY THAT REFUSES TO COMPLY WITH A LOWER COURT ORDER." STOLTENBERG V. AMPTON INVESTMENTS (2013) 215 CAL.APP.4TH 1225, 1229. THERE IS NO BASIS TO STRIKE THE GENERAL DENIAL UNDER CCP 435 AND 436. IF DEFENDANT FAILS TO COMPLY WITH DISCOVERY IN THIS CASE, THEN PLAINTIFF MAY PURSUE ITS REMEDIES UNDER THE CALIFORNIA CIVIL DISCOVERY ACT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: SUZANNE BOLANOS; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/SB

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT ENTREPENEUR MEDIA INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE VERIFIED CROSS-COMPLAINT OF DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT SCOTT SMITH - PLAINTIFF'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS' ABUSE OF PROCESS CROSS-CLAIM IS BARRED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE UNDER CIVIL CODE SECTION 47(C). DEFENDANT'S INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIM IS UNLIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. HARASSING EMAILS SENT IN THE COURSE OF LITIGATION, SUCH AS THOSE PLAINTIFF RECEIVED, ARE INJURIOUS BUT NOT EXTREME AND OUTRAGEOUS UNDER COCHRAN V. COCHRAN (1998) 65 CAL. APP. 4TH 491. FURTHERMORE, DEFENDANT'S INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIM ARGUABLY DOES NOT ARISE OUT OF CONSTITUTIONALLY PROTECTED ACTIVITY BECAUSE IT IS BASED ON THREATENING BEHAVIOR. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. ORDER SUSTAINING OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE ALSO SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ENTREPENEUR MEDIA INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE GENERAL DENIAL - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANT'S ANSWER IS DENIED. "THE AMENDED COMPLAINT FURNISHES THE SOLE BASIS FOR THE CAUSE OF ACTION, AND THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT CEASES TO HAVE ANY EFFECT EITHER AS A PLEADING OR AS A BASIS FOR JUDGMENT. (STATE COMP. INS. FUND V. SUPERIOR COURT (2010) 184 CAL.APP.4TH 1124, 1131.) " ' "[A]N AMENDATORY PLEADING SUPERCEDES THE ORIGINAL ONE, WHICH CEASES TO PERFORM ANY FUNCTION AS A PLEADING." ' " (FOREMAN & CLARK CORP. V. FALLON (1971) 3 CAL.3D 875, 884.) HERE, PLAINTIFF FILED ITS MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANT'S ANSWER ON NOVEMBER 5, 2015. DEFENDANT FILED AN ANSWER TO PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED COMPLAINT ON NOVEMBER 22, 2015. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION CANNOT STRIKE DEFENDANT'S ANSWER TO PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED COMPLAINT, BECAUSE DEFENDANT FILED HIS ANSWER TO PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED COMPLAINT AFTER PLAINTIFF FILED ITS MOTION. THE PREVALING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC.'S MOTION TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED AND/OR COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS - FIRST SET ALTHOUGH SMITH'S BEHAVIOR IN DISCOVERY LACKED DILIGENCE AND HAS RESULTED IN SIGNIFICANT COSTS TO BOTH EMI AND THE COURT, THE MOTION HERE, MUST NONETHELESS BE DENIED AS MOOT. FURTHER, BECAUSE EMI DECLINED TO REQUEST MONETARY SANCTIONS, FAILED TO NOTICE SANCTIONS IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION, FAILED TO NOTE THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS ON THE CAPTION PAGE, FAILED TO SUPPORT ANY REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS WITH FACTS IN DECLARATION, AND FAILED TO ARGUE FOR SANCTIONS IN MEMORANDUM, THE PREVAILING PARTY EMI, SHOULD RECOVER NO SANCTIONS, AND SANCTIONS AS AGAINST ALL PARTIES SHOULD BE DENIED. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SCOTT SMITH'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO HIS SECOND AND THIRD SETS OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND HIS THIRD SET OF DOCUMENT REQUESTS IS GRANTED IN PART. NO LATER THAN MAY 30, 2017 PLAINTIFF ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA, INC. MUST PROVIDE VERIFIED RESPONSES WITHOUT ANY OBJECTIONS TO THE FOLLOWING THREE CONTENTION INTERROGATORIES: 1) STATE ALL FACTS THAT SUPPORT EACH OF THE ALLEGATIONS MADE IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT; 2) IDENTIFY BY NAME AND LAST KNOWN ADDRESS AND PHONE NUMBER EACH PERSON WHO KNOWS THOSE FACTS AND WHAT FACTS EACH PERSON KNOWS; AND 3) IDENTIFY ALL DOCUMENTS THAT WERE WRITTEN PRIOR TO THE INITIATION OF THIS LAWSUIT THAT REFER OR REFLECT SUCH FACTS. NO LATER THAN MAY 18, 2017 PLAINTIFF MUST PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS SO IDENTIFIED OR LIST THEM IN A PRIVILEGE LOG AND PROVIDE A VERIFICATION STATING THAT ALL DOCUMENTS THAT WERE WRITTEN PRIOR TO THE INITIATION OF THIS LAWSUIT THAT REFER OR REFLECT ANY OF THE FACTS THAT SUPPORT ANY OF THE ALLEGATIONS MADE IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT HAVE EITHER BEEN PRODUCED TO MR. SMITH OR LISTED ON A PRIVILEGE LOG. BY RESPONDING TO THE FOREGOING, PLAINTIFF WILL HAVE PROVIDED ALL THE DISCOVERY THAT MR. SMITH IS ENTITLED TO RECEIVE IN RESPONSE TO HIS SECOND AND THIRD SETS OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND HIS THIRD SET OF DOCUMENT REQUESTS. 4) PLAINTIFF CAN IDENTIFY DOCUMENTS WHICH ARE PUBLICLY AVAILABLE IN THE PUBLIC OFFICE BUT NOT PRODUCE. LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANT SCOTT SMITH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND-OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE FIRST AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA, INC. ARE DENIED IN THEIR ENTIRETY. EMI SUBMITTED ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER THE DOMAIN NAMES MR. SMITH TRANSFERRED HAD ANY VALUE WHEN HE TRANSFERRED THEM AND WHETHER MR. SMITH MADE THE TRANSFERS WITH THE INTENT TO HINDER, DELAY OR DEFRAUD EMI IN ITS EFFORTS TO COLLECT ON THE JUDGMENT IT HAS AGAINST MR. SMITH. THE DECLARATION OF DOUGLAS BANIA STATES THAT THE DOMAIN NAMES EACH HAD A VALUE OF BETWEEN $550 AND $2,500 AT THE TIME THEY WERE TRANSFERRED TO MS. MIX AND THAT MR. SMITH DID NOT TRANSFER THE DOMAIN NAMES IN RETURN FOR THEIR FAIR MARKET VALUE. MR. SMITH'S SALE OF THE DOMAIN NAMES TO HIS GIRLFRIEND FOR LESS THAN THEIR VALUE CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO HIS INTENT. THE BANKRUPTCY COURT'S DECISION DOES NOT COLLATERALLY ESTOP EMI'S CLAIMS BECAUSE THAT DECISION WAS MADE IN 2007 AND THE DOMAIN NAMES WERE NOT TRANSFERRED UNTIL 2012. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KAREN MIX'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO VACATE ENTRY DEFAULT AND FOR AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART. AS BEST AS THE COURT CAN DISCERN, MS. MIX'S PRIMARY CONCERN IS THAT SHE BE PERMITTED TO CHALLENGE SOME OR ALL OF THE COSTS CLAIMED BY PLAINTIFF ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA, INC. UNDER THE UNIQUE CIRCUMSTANCES OF THIS CASE AND TO AVOID A POTENTIAL MISCARRIAGE OF JUSTICE, THE COURT AGREES AND RE-CHARACTERIZES MS. MIX'S MOTION AS A MOTION TO STRIKE OR TAX COSTS WHICH WILL BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 302 ON FEBRUARY 13, 2018. MS. MIX MAY FILE AN INITIAL BRIEF STATING WHAT COSTS SHE SEEKS TO HAVE STRICKEN OR TAXED AND WHY NO LATER THAN JANUARY 19, 2018. ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA MAY FILE AN OPPOSITION BRIEF NO LATER THAN JANUARY 30, 2018. MS. MIX MAY FILE A REPLY BRIEF NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 6, 2018. ALL OTHER PORTIONS OF MS. MIX'S MOTION ARE DENIED. MS. MIX HAS FAILED TO SHOW GROUNDS TO SUPPORT QUASHING SERVICE OF SUMMONS ON HER, VACATING THE DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST HER OR IMPOSING SANCTIONS ON ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD E. LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-COMPLAINANT SCOTT SMITH'S MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO CCP 128.5 AND 128.7. DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT SCOTT SMITH'S MOTIONS FOR SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTIONS 128.5 AND 128.7 AGAINST CROSS-DEFENDANT ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA, INC. (EMI) AND ITS ATTORNEY, DAVID J. COOK, ARE DENIED. BECAUSE A SELF-REPRESENTED LITIGANT DOES NOT "INCUR" ATTORNEYS' FEES, HE OR SHE MAY NOT RECOVER AN AWARD OF ATTORNEYS' FEES AS SANCTIONS. (MUSAELIAN V. ADAMS (2009) 45 CAL.4TH 512, 520.) FURTHER, MR. SMITH CANNOT RECOVER EXPENSES OTHER THAN ATTORNEYS' FEES AS SANCTIONS EITHER, AS "[A] PARTY WHO ACTS ON HIS OR HER OWN BEHALF DOES NOT THEREBY GENERATE AN EXPENSE THAT THE PARTY HAS BECOME OBLIGATED TO PAY." (ID. AT 517.) (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT). ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Plaintiff'S Motion For Reconsideration (Per Order Filed 6/10/19)
Off Calendar Jul-01-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. DAVID J. COOK, ESQ. of Cook Collection Attorneys PLC (Tel: 415-989-4730) appearing for plaintiff Entrepeneur Media Inc.;
Defendant SCOTT SMITH (Tel: 916-453-8611) appearing in pro per.

Plaintiff's Motion for Reconsideration.

The matter is argued and submitted by the parties. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant and Cross-Complainant Scott Smith's motion for reconsideration of the Court's March 15, 2019 order denying Defendant's motion for sanctions under Code of Civil Procedure sections 128.5 and 128.7 is denied, and the Court declines Mr. Smith's invitation to reconsider the order on its own motion pursuant to Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal.4th 1094. None of the purported new evidence Mr. Smith seeks to present affects the basis for the Court's original ruling, i.e., that a self-represented litigant may not recover an award of attorneys' fees or other expenses as sanctions. (Musaelian v. Adams (2009) 45 Cal.4th 512, 517, 520.) The Court acknowledges that Code of Civil Procedure section 128.5(f) and 128.7(d) provide that sanctions awarded under those sections may consist of, or include, directives of a nonmonetary nature or an order to pay a penalty into court. However, Mr. Smith did not seek the imposition of any non-monetary sanctions. In any event, the Court has reviewed Mr. Smith's voluminous declaration and exhibits, and finds that Mr. Smith has not met his burden to show that EMI and its counsel have engaged in conduct warranting the imposition of sanctions, whether payable to Mr. Smith or to the Court. The fact that Mr. Jurin filed a declaration adverse to Mr. Smith in an adversary proceeding in bankruptcy court in 2006, or that he was once seen "in the company of EMI's counsel David J. Cook" (Smith Decl. 11, 14 & Ex. I), does not establish, as Mr. Smith alleges, that EMI and Mr. Cook "have for years colluded with" Mr. Jurin, engaged in "abusive tactics" against Mr. Smith, or committed perjury, nor does Mr. Cook's silence in the face of Mr. Smith's accusations constitute an admission of such collusion or other improper conduct. The Court notes, however, that its denial of the original motion and of reconsideration is without prejudice to Mr. Smith raising any of the issues concerning EMI and its counsel's conduct in connection with his pending claim for abuse of process, or from seeking to recover any asserted expenses or fees as damages on that claim.

The Court signs a formal order.

The Court asks the parties to voluntarily comply to meet and confer in good faith in person or via telephone regarding the subject of any future motions and hearing dates. The Court asks that the parties refrain from including ad hominem content in any future motion papers as not being productive. The Court indicates a failure to do so may result in the Court's decision to take a party's motion off calendar.

JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM (302/EPS)

MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
David J. Cook, esq. (415-989-4730) for Plaintiff.
Scott Smith (916-453-8611) Defendant in propria persona via court call.

The Court announces its tentative ruling.
RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-COMPLAINANT SCOTT SMITH's MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS pursuant to CCP 128.5 and 128.7.

Defendant and Cross-Complainant Scott Smith's motions for sanctions pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 128.5 and 128.7 against Cross-Defendant Entrepreneur Media, Inc. (EMI) and its attorney, David J. Cook, are denied. Because a self-represented litigant does not "incur" attorneys' fees, he or she may not recover an award of attorneys' fees as sanctions. (Musaelian v. Adams (2009) 45 Cal.4th 512, 520.) Further, Mr. Smith cannot recover expenses other than attorneys' fees as sanctions either, as "[a] party who acts on his or her own behalf does not thereby generate an expense that the party has become obligated to pay." (Id. at 517.) (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript).

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss The Remaining Roe Defendants Of The Cross-Complaint Pursuant To C.C.P. Sections 583.210, 583.240, And 583.250; And Motion To Vacate Protective Order, Or, In The Alternative, To Sever Claims Against Roe Defendants Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 1048(B)
Off Calendar Jan-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Cook, Esq. 415-989-4730 of Cook collection Attorneys for Plaintiff.
Scott Smith, Esq. 916-453-8611 Defendant in Pro Per through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court advises parties it did not receive a "contest" email. Plaintiff argues no stay of proceedings, cites Graf case. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss The Remaining Roe Defendants Of The Cross-Complaint Pursuant To C.C.P. Sections 583.210, 583.240, And 583.250; And Motion To Vacate Protective Order, Or, In The Alternative, To Sever Claims Against Roe Defendants Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 1048(B).

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff Entrepreneur Media, Inc.'s motion to dismiss the remaining Roe defendants of the cross-complaint and to vacate the order filed December 11, 2018 granting is denied. Because the purpose of the Google subpoena was to enable cross-complainant Scott Smith to identify the Roe cross-defendants, the September 4, 2018 order was, in practical effect and in law, a stay of this action as to the Roe cross-defendants that affected service on the Roe cross-defendants per CCP 583.240(b). Moreover, the September 4, 2018 order made service on the Roe cross-defendants impossible, impracticable or futile due to a cause beyond Mr. Smith's control per CCP 583.240(d). The September 4, 2019 order was not vacated till December 11, 2018, so the time period between those dates must be excluded from the calculation of the three year mandatory service period. EMI's arguments that there should be a shorter period excluded lack merit.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Cross-Complainant Scott Smiths Motion And Notice Of Motion To Vacate Protective Order, Or, In The Alternative, Sever Roe Defendants Pursuant To C.C.P. 1048b
Off Calendar Dec-11-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

David j. Cook, Esq., Cook collection Atroneys PLC, Telephone 415-989-4730, appearing for Plaintiff,
Scott R. smith, Telephone 916-453-8611, appearing in Pro Se by CourtCall;

Ruling - A R G U E D. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Cross-complainant Scott Smith's motion to vacate protective order and to serve a subpoena on Google to determine the true identity of the person(s) sending emails under the name of James Johnson is granted. The order filed September 4, 2018 granting cross-defendant Entrepreneur Media, Inc.'s ex parte motion for a protective order is now vacated and Mr. Smith is granted permission to take all necessary steps to serve his proposed subpoena on Google. Even if this motion were construed as a motion for reconsideration of the order filed September 4, 2018 and did not comply with the requirements of CCP 1008, the court treats this motion as an invitation to reconsider the September 4, 2018 per Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 1094 and accepts the invitation. Mr. Smith has shown good cause for the service of the subpoena on Google. Because the subpoena seeks to learn the identity of the Roe cross-defendants to enable Mr. Smith to prosecute his cross-claims against the Roe cross-defendants and is not sought to pursue any claims against EMI, the subpoena is not barred by the pendency of the appeal of this court's order granting EMI's anti-SLAPP motion as to Mr. Smith's cross-claims against EMI or the statute barring discovery against a party who filed an anti-SLAPP motion. No ruling by the Court of Appeal will affect Mr. Smith's cross-claims against the Roe cross-defendants and thus the subpoena is not embraced by the pending appeal. Notwithstanding EMI's prior evidentiary submissions denying any connection to the emails sent under the name of James Johnson, the possibility that the subpoena to Google may reveal information that might be relevant to Mr. Smith's cross-claims against EMI in the event that Mr. Smith is ever permitted to pursue those cross-claims does not make the subpoena impermissible at this time. Mr. Smith has shown good cause for the service of the subpoena on Google and none of EMI's arguments opposing the service of the subpoena are persuasive. Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs Etc.
Off Calendar Jan-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Cook, Esq. 415-989-4730 of Cook Collection Attorneys for Plaintiff.
Scott Smith 916-453-8611 Defendant/Cross-Complainant in Pro Per through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court shares his views with the parties. Plaintiff argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's Motion To Tax Costs Pursuant to CCP section 1033.5 and CRC 3.1700(b)(1).

Plaintiff Entrepreneur Media, Inc.'s motion to tax costs is granted in small part. The following claimed costs are taxed: 1) $150 of the filing and motion fees because of an addition error by defendant Scott Smith; 2) All $795.43 of the service of process costs because no explanation is provided why the costs were incurred and it is not apparent why such costs were incurred and/or the costs are not allowed per the costs statute; and 3) All $120 of the court reporter fees because no explanation is provided why the costs were incurred and it is not apparent why such costs were incurred and/or the costs are not allowed per the costs statute. All other costs sought to be taxed are allowed. All such costs are adequately documented and/or the reasons why the costs were incurred are apparent to the court and no additional explanation or documentation is necessary. All other arguments why such costs should be taxed lack merit. The total amount of taxed costs is $1,065.43. Subtracting that amount from the total claimed costs of $14,175.18 results in a costs award in favor of Mr. Smith and against Entrepreneur Media of $13,109.75. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, mtorreano@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)

Off Calendar Jan-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Cook, Esq. 415-989-4730 of Cook Collection Attorneys for Plaintiff.
Scott Smith 916-453-8611 Defendant/Cross-Complainant in Pro Per through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court shares his views with the parties. Plaintiff argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's Motion To Offset Any Costs That The Court Might Award In Favor Of Scott Smith To And Against, To The Extent Awarded, The Judgment In Favor Of Entrepreneur Media, Inc. Entered In The Usdc Cd Cal., And Application Of Any Possible Cost Award As An Offset Against Emis Lien Under Ccp Section 708.470(A); And Stay Of Enforcement Under The Cost Award Under Ccp Section 708.440(A).

The court re-affirms the following tentative ruling issued on December 18, 2017: Plaintiff Entrepreneur Media, Inc.'s motion to offset any costs that might be awarded in favor of defendant Scott Smith against its lien is denied. Per Margott v. Gem Properties, Inc. (1973) 34 Cal. App. 3d 849, 856 and CCP 708.470(a), the court exercises its discretion to require Entrepreneur Media to pay the full amount of the costs awarded to Mr. Scott directly to him rather than as an offset against the debt Mr. Smith owes to it. Equitable principles -- including but not limited to those underlying such concepts as unclean hands, abuse of process and avoidance of bullying - demand that Entrepreneur Media be required to pay out of its pocket all of Mr. Smith's allowable costs rather than use those costs to reduce a debt that will likely never be paid in full where, as here, Entrepreneur Media engaged in abusive and objectively irrational over-litigation of near-worthless claims for the sole apparent purpose of causing Mr. Smith to incur costs and suffer burdens.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, mtorreano@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)

After arguments, the Court adopts the tentative ruling in all respects, as follows: DEFENDANT KAREN MIX'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint Or, In The Alternative, To Vacate Entry Default And For An Order To Show Cause Re Sanctions is GRANTED IN PART. Under the unique circumstances of this case and to avoid a potential miscarriage of justice, the court agrees and re-characterizes Ms. Mix's motion as a motion to strike or tax costs which will be heard in department 302 on February 13, 2018. Ms. Mix may file an initial brief stating what costs she seeks to have stricken or taxed and why no later than January 19, 2018. Entrepreneur Media may file an opposition brief no later than January 30, 2018. Ms. Mix may file a reply brief no later than February 6, 2018. All other portions of Ms. Mix's motion are denied. Ms. Mix has failed to show grounds to support quashing service of summons on her, vacating the default entered against her or imposing sanctions on Entrepreneur Media.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And-Or Summary Adjudication On The First And Fourth Causes Of Action Of The First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-28-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, David J. Cook of the law offices of Cook Collection Attorneys PLC (415.989.4730) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Scott R. Smith (916.453.8611) appears via telephone as self-represented defendant/cross-complainant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Plaintiff's objections are overruled. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT SCOTT SMITH's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And-Or Summary Adjudication On The First And Fourth Causes Of Action Of The First Amended Complaint filed by plaintiff Entrepreneur Media, Inc. are denied in their entirety. EMI submitted admissible evidence showing that there are triable issues whether the domain names Mr. Smith transferred had any value when he transferred them and whether Mr. Smith made the transfers with the intent to hinder, delay or defraud EMI in its efforts to collect on the judgment it has against Mr. Smith. The declaration of Douglas Bania states that the domain names each had a value of between $550 and $2,500 at the time they were transferred to Ms. Mix and that Mr. Smith did not transfer the domain names in return for their fair market value. Mr. Smith's sale of the domain names to his girlfriend for less than their value creates a triable issue as to his intent. The Bankruptcy Court's decision does not collaterally estop EMI's claims because that decision was made in 2007 and the domain names were not transferred until 2012. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint Etc.
Off Calendar Jun-28-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Cook, Esq. (415-989-4730) of Cook Collections for Plaintiff.
Scott Smith (916-453-8611) for Defendant in Pro Per through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC.'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint for Creditor's Suit Under CCP 708.210 et. seq., action on fraudulent conveyances, and appointment of Receiver is DENIED. At this stage of the case, the proposed new claim for declaratory relief serves no useful purpose and will likely result in further litigation of this already egregiously over-litigated case. All relief sought by plaintiff can be obtained by its existing claims. This motion seems to be precipitated by the court's comments at the hearing on plaintiff's motion for summary judgment, but there is now insufficient time for plaintiff to file another motion for summary judgment or adjudication. In all events, defendant Scott Smith does not deny that he transferred the domain names to Ms. Mix, as he stated at the hearing on May 10, 2017. The motion is denied per CCP 1061 which provides that the "court may refuse to exercise the power granted by this chapter [concerning declaratory relief] in any case where its declaration or determination is not necessary or proper at the time under all the circumstances." (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Order is signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT On All Causes Of Action And/Or Summary Adjudication On All Causes Of Action Pursuant To C.C.P. 437c(A)(1)&(F)(1) Based Upon The First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-10-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAVID J. COOK OF COOK COLLECTION ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-989-4730 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
SCOTT SMITH, TEL. NO. 1-916-453-8611, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER;

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Entrepreneur Media, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or summary adjudication as to all four causes of action against defendant Scott Smith is denied in its entirety. As a preliminary matter, the court notes that, while the notice of motion states that this motion is directed to all four causes of action in the first amended complaint, the FAC shows that only the first and fourth causes of action are alleged against Mr. Smith. Liberally construing the FAC, the first and fourth causes of action allege claims against Mr. Smith for fraudulent transfer of two domain names by Mr. Smith based on intent to hinder, delay or defraud (CACI 4200) and constructive fraud (CACI 4202 and 4203). Entrepreneur's argument that Mr. Smith lacks "standing" to oppose this motion based upon the default of co-defendant Ms. Mix lacks merit. Mr. Smith has standing to oppose the motion because Entrepreneur has sued him personally and seeks a judgment or adjudication, which could include costs of suit, against him. Whether or not Mr. Smith is a necessary party to this action is a different and not relevant issue since Mr. Smith has been named as a defendant in this action. There is nothing that prevents plaintiff from dismissing Mr. Smith and seeking a default judgment against Ms. Mix. A party seeking summary adjudication must present evidence proving each element of its cause of action. (See CCP 437c(p)(1).) Entrepreneur fails to maintain its initial burden of production because it does not present any evidence showing that it was harmed by the alleged fraudulent transfers of the domain names. Per CACI 4200, 4202 and 4203, an essential element of each of the fraudulent transfer claims is that the plaintiff was harmed by the transfer. "A well-established principle of the law of fraudulent transfers is, 'A transfer in fraud of creditors may be attacked only by one who is injured thereby. Mere intent to delay or defraud is not sufficient; injury to the creditor must be shown affirmatively. In other words, prejudice to the plaintiff is essential. It cannot be said that a creditor has been injured unless the transfer puts beyond [her] reach property [she] otherwise would be able to subject to the payment of [her] debt.'" (Mehrtash v. Mehrtash (2001) 93 Cal.App.4th 75, 80.) Entrepreneur also fails to present any admissible evidence that Mr. Smith intended to hinder, delay, or defraud Entrepreneur's collection efforts. Mr. Smith's appellate brief does not show such hindrance. Nor has Entrepreneur provided admissible evidence that Mr. Smith did not receive reasonably equivalent value for the transfers or that the domain names have value. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

Cross-Defendant Entrepeneur Media Inc'S Motion To Strike Verified Cross-Complaint Of Defendant And Cross-Complainant Scott Smith
Off Calendar Jan-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Cook and Ryan Dahm of Cook Collection Attorneys PLC telephone number 415-989-4730 for Plaintiff.
Scott Smith telephone number 916-453-8611 Defendant in Propria Persona appeared by telephone.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. Cross-Defendant Entrepeneur Media Inc's Motion To Strike Verified Cross-Complaint Of Defendant And Cross-Complainant Scott Smith - Plaintiff's Special Motion to Strike is Granted. Defendants' abuse of process cross-claim is barred by the litigation privilege under Civil Code section 47(c). Defendant's Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress claim is unlikely to prevail on the merits. Harassing emails sent in the course of litigation, such as those Plaintiff received, are injurious but not extreme and outrageous under Cochran v. Cochran (1998) 65 Cal. App. 4th 491. Furthermore, Defendant's Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress claim arguably does not arise out of constitutionally protected activity because it is based on threatening behavior. Order signed in open Court. Order sustaining objections to evidence also signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Plaintiff Entrepeneur Media Inc'S Motion To Strike General Denial
Off Calendar Jan-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Cook and Ryan Dahm of Cook Collection Attorneys PLC telephone number 415-989-4730 for Plaintiff.
Scott Smith telephone number 916-453-8611 Defendant in Propria Persona appeared by telephone.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tenative ruling.
Plaintiff Entrepeneur Media Inc's Motion To Strike General Denial - Plaintiff's Motion to Strike Defendant's Answer is Denied. "The amended complaint furnishes the sole basis for the cause of action, and the original complaint ceases to have any effect either as a pleading or as a basis for judgment. (State Comp. Ins. Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1131.) " ' "[A]n amendatory pleading supercedes the original one, which ceases to perform any function as a pleading." ' " (Foreman & Clark Corp. v. Fallon (1971) 3 Cal.3d 875, 884.) Here, Plaintiff filed its Motion to Strike Defendant's Answer on November 5, 2015. Defendant filed an answer to Plaintiff's amended complaint on November 22, 2015. Plaintiff's motion cannot strike Defendant's answer to Plaintiff's amended complaint, because Defendant filed his answer to Plaintiff's amended complaint after Plaintiff filed its motion. The prevaling party is to provide a form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RYAN L. DAHM OF COOK COLLECTIONS ATTORNEYS. TEL. NO. 1-415-989-4730 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
SCOTT, SMITH, TEL. NO. 1-916-453-8611, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT TO ADD ADDITIONAL PARTY AND CAUSES OF ACTION is GRANTED. Leave to amend a complaint should be liberally granted when no prejudice is shown to the adverse party. (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 761. Defendant fails to show any prejudice. Defendant's appeal of the Court's denial of his anti-SLAPP motion stayed the matter "on the merits" until July 2, 2015, the date of the remittitur. (See Varian Medical Systems, Inc. v. Delfino (2005) 35 Cal.4th 180, 194.) However, Defendant supplies no authority for the proposition that accepting the filing on June 12, 2015 was an action "on the merits" in excess of the Court's jurisdiction. The August 17, 2015 hearing date for this matter is more than 16 court days after July 2, 2015, the date of remittitur. The Court sees no barrier to deciding the motion now. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Under C.C.P 436
Off Calendar Jul-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC.'S Motion To Strike Under C.C.P 436 is denied. Plaintiff relies on an inapposite appellate doctrine. "An appellate court has the inherent power, under the 'disentitlement doctrine,' to dismiss an appeal by a party that refuses to comply with a lower court order." Stoltenberg v. Ampton Investments (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 1225, 1229. There is no basis to strike the general denial under CCP 435 and 436. If defendant fails to comply with discovery in this case, then plaintiff may pursue its remedies under the California Civil Discovery Act. Order signed. MINI MINUTES:

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SCOTT SMITH's Anti-Slapp Motion to Strike is denied. Defendant fails to show that plaintiff's complaint is based on petitioning or other First Amendment activities. Defendant has not carried his burden with regard to prong one of CCP 425.16. The gravamen of plaintiff's suit is to unwind an allegedly fraudulent transfer. The allegations in paragraphs 10 and 13 in the Complaint are incidental and not a basis for liability. See Martinez v. Metabolife Intern., Inc., (2003) 113 Cal.App.4th 181, 188. The request for sanctions is denied. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
